assertions.expectation: Uses 'no upgrades' exclusion (30-day max) for the BFCM period — blocks everything including patches
assertions.reason: Response uses ALL_UPGRADES scope for BFCM but doesn't specify it as 'no upgrades' exclusion type or mention the 30-day maximum limit

assertions.expectation: Uses 'no upgrades' exclusion for the June code freeze period
assertions.reason: Response uses ALL_UPGRADES scope for June but doesn't specify it as 'no upgrades' exclusion type or mention the 30-day maximum limit

assertions.expectation: Uses 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion for ongoing minor upgrade control — allows CP patches but blocks disruptive changes
assertions.reason: Response doesn't provide a 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion type for ongoing minor upgrade control; only shows ALL_UPGRADES exclusions

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for 'no upgrades' exclusions vs up-to-EoS for 'no minor or node upgrades'
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the 30-day maximum limitation for 'no upgrades' exclusions or the up-to-EoS duration for 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusions

assertions.expectation: Provides specific gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope flags for each exclusion type
assertions.reason: Response provides gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope ALL_UPGRADES flags

assertions.expectation: Notes that you can have a maximum of three 'no upgrades' exclusions, and they must allow 48h of maintenance in any 32-day window
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the maximum of three 'no upgrades' exclusions or the 48h maintenance requirement in any 32-day window

assertions.expectation: Mentions that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explicitly mention that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows while auto-upgrades respect them

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during extended exclusion periods
assertions.reason: Response doesn't warn about the risks of falling behind on versions during extended exclusion periods
